brief_title: Open-label Leucovorin Pharmacokinetic Study in Patients Receiving High Dose Methotrexate With or Without Voraxaze
official_title: An Open-Label Study to Assess the Pharmacokinetics of Leucovorin in Patients Receiving High Dose Methotrexate, With or Without Voraxaze Treatment
brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to investigate whether the administration of Voraxaze reduces exposure to leucovorin and its active metabolite to below the level achieved in patients who have not received Voraxaze.

interventions.name: high-dose methotrexate, leucovorin
interventions.description: standard of care, leucovorin every 6 hours

eligibility.criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Receiving High-dose methotrexate (HDMTX) with or without risk of methotrexate (MTX) toxicity, impaired renal function, and delayed MTX elimination
* Require intravenous leucovorin

Exclusion Criteria:

* Arm A only: allergic reactions to lactose
* Arm A only: hereditary fructose or galactose intolerance
* Arm B only: delayed elimination of MTX

primary_outcomes.timeFrame: 5 minutes, 30 minutes, 1 hour, 2 hours and 3 hours post-LV administration
primary_outcomes.description: Geometric mean (6S)-leucovorin area under the plasma concentration vs. time curve from time 0 to the 3-hour time point.
